thinks.think: Several small, translucent spheres rest on a gray, textured surface. A large, dark, jagged mass suddenly impacts the ground, dominating the foreground. A yellow graphical overlay consisting of a horizontal line and vertical bar appears superimposed over the dark object as it lands. The impact obscures the previous view of the scattered spheres.

White text reading "La chaine YouTube" remains above a yellow horizontal line as the large dark foreground mass disappears. Below the line, the text "Zone Pétanque présente" becomes fully visible. The background returns to showing scattered translucent spheres resting on the gray, textured gravel surface behind the static graphical overlay.

A large, dark spherical object rolls into view from the right side, positioning itself behind the static yellow graphical overlay. The white text "La chaine YouTube" and "Zone Pétanque présente" remains fixed in the center. The background continues to show scattered translucent spheres on the gray gravel surface as the dark ball settles near the text.

The white text and horizontal yellow line fade out completely. A dark silhouette of a person's upper body and head solidifies in the center foreground, facing away. The large dark sphere on the right disappears. A yellow vertical bar remains on the right edge. The background gravel and scattered spheres are still visible around the dark figure.

The yellow vertical bar on the right disappears. The central dark silhouette clarifies into a standing figure with hands on hips. Large white text reading "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" appears across the center, superimposed over the dark figure and the gravel background containing scattered spheres.

The large white text "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" undergoes a glitch effect, duplicating slightly, while smaller white text "très précis" fades in at the center. The dark silhouette figure behind the text changes pose, leaning forward as the background gravel and spheres remain constant.

The dark silhouette clarifies into a figure wearing a cap, captured mid-throw with an arm extended backward. Glitched white text reading "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" and "très précis" remain superimposed over the center. The background of gray gravel and scattered spheres persists behind the action.

The glitched text fades, replaced by bold white text reading "TIRS DE RAFI" centered over a dark silhouette of a person's upper body. The figure faces forward, obscuring the gravel background, while a yellow vertical bar appears to the left of the new text overlay.

The previous silhouette is replaced by a close-up of a man's face. A graphic overlay with yellow, black, and white chevrons dominates the right side. A lower banner displays "NORDINE ZOUAK" in white capital letters, while faint text above reads "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2004" and a small logo appears in the top left corner.

The scene remains static, displaying a man's face on the left alongside a graphic overlay of yellow, black, and white chevrons on the right. White text reading "NORDINE ZOUAK" stays at the bottom, while "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2004" and a "ZONE PETANQUE" logo persist in their respective positions.

The introductory graphic transitions to a high-angle view of a gravel pétanque court. Three silver metal boules and a small green jack rest on the ground near a diagonal white boundary line. The 'ZONE PETANQUE' logo persists in the upper left corner of the screen.

The camera cuts from the high-angle court view to a medium shot of a man with short dark hair. He wears a light tan button-down shirt and a gold necklace with a pendant. He stands facing forward against a blurred background of spectators. The white "ZONE PETANQUE" logo remains fixed in the upper left corner.

The medium shot continues to feature the man in the light tan button-down shirt and gold necklace facing forward. He remains relatively stationary against the blurred background of spectators. The white "ZONE PETANQUE" logo persists in the upper left corner throughout the duration.

The scene cuts to a wide angle of the pétanque court, revealing scattered metal boules on the sand near a white line. Spectators line the background behind a metal barrier, alongside a blue banner displaying "Marionnaud." The "ZONE PETANQUE" logo persists in the upper left corner.

The scene maintains a wide view of the gravel court, tracking slightly rightward along the white diagonal line. Several silver boules lie stationary on the ground. Behind the metal crowd barrier, spectators watch the game, and the channel logo remains fixed in the upper left.

The view settles on a white boundary line forming a corner on the gravel court. Two silver boules lie near the vertex. To the right, two people stand observing; one wears dark shorts with sandals, the other brown shorts and sneakers. Spectators remain behind a metal barrier, and the channel logo persists top left.

The scene cuts to a wide shot showing a man in a green shirt and dark shorts walking away across the gravel court. Behind him, a dense crowd of spectators stands behind a metal barrier, with a banner partially reading "La Marseill" visible in the background. The channel logo remains in the top left corner.

The man in the green shirt and dark shorts continues walking away across the gravel court towards the right. Spectators remain behind the metal barrier in the background, while another individual in a white shirt stands near the fence. The channel logo persists in the top left corner.

The man in the green shirt continues walking away across the gravel court towards the right. Another man wearing a white t-shirt and dark shorts walks from right to left near the spectator barrier. Finally, a cross-fade effect begins, superimposing a close-up of a man's face over the court scene.

The scene transitions fully to a close-up of a man in a light tan shirt leaning forward, his gaze fixed ahead as if preparing to throw. Blurred spectators fill the background behind him, while the white "ZONE PETANQUE" logo stays fixed in the upper left corner.

A man in a light tan shirt extends his arm forward, finishing a throwing motion. The view cuts to a high-angle shot of the gravel court where three silver boules and a small green jack lie near a white line. The white Zone Pétanque logo is visible in the upper left corner.

A high-angle view shows a gravel court with three silver boules and a green jack near a white line. A new silver boule rolls into the frame from the top right area, moving toward the center cluster. The white Zone Pétanque logo is visible in the upper left corner throughout the shot.

A silver boule rolls rapidly across the upper right section of the gravel court. Immediately after, the upper area becomes empty, indicating the previous top silver boule has been knocked away. Only the small green jack and two stationary silver boules remain visible near the bottom white boundary line.

A high-angle static shot displays the gravel court surface with a white boundary line. A small green jack sits near the line, while two silver boules rest near the bottom edge. The upper section of the court is clear, and the white Zone Pétanque logo is visible in the top left corner.

The view cuts to a close-up of a man wearing dark sunglasses. A graphic overlay featuring yellow, black, and white chevrons slides in from the right. Text overlays identify the event as "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2020" and the player as "PATRICE GREGORI" in large white font at the bottom.

A close-up shot persists featuring a man wearing dark sunglasses. Graphic overlays with yellow, black, and white chevrons remain on the right side. Text identifying the event "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2020" and player "PATRICE GREGORI" stays visible at the bottom, while the Zone Pétanque logo remains in the top left corner.

The scene cuts to a wide shot of a sunlit pétanque court with spectators in bleachers. Three players in white shirts and shorts stand on the gravel; one is left, two are right. Metal boules lie near the bottom right. A scoreboard graphic is top left, and a channel logo is top right.

In a wide shot of the sunlit court, a man in a white shirt and dark shorts on the far left crouches down near the white boundary line. Two other men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right side, while spectators fill the bleachers in the background under a blue sky.

A man in a white shirt and dark shorts remains crouched near the left white boundary line on the gravel court. Two other men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right. Spectators fill the tiered seating in the background under a bright sky, while three metal boules rest on the ground.

The camera cuts to a close-up of a man with a grey beard and dark sunglasses looking downward. He wears a white collared shirt. The Zone Pétanque logo is visible in the top left corner, and a channel logo with the number 3 appears in the top right corner against a blurred background.

The close-up shot persists, featuring a man with a grey beard and dark sunglasses looking downward. He wears a white collared shirt. The Zone Pétanque logo remains in the top left corner, and a channel logo with the number 3 is visible in the top right against a blurred background.

The camera cuts to a wide shot of the sunlit gravel court. A man in a white shirt and dark shorts crouches on the left side, preparing to throw. Two men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right side. Three metal boules lie on the ground in the foreground near the bottom right corner.

The wide shot continues with the man in a white shirt and dark shorts crouching on the left side of the gravel court. Two men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right. Three metal boules lie stationary on the ground in the foreground. Spectators fill the tiered seating in the background under a bright sky.

A high-angle shot shows gray gravel with two dark metal boules resting on the ground. Shadows of observers' legs appear at the top edge. A white boundary line runs diagonally on the right. Zone Pétanque and number 3 logos are visible.

The high-angle camera pans across the gray gravel court, showing dark metal boules resting on the ground. Shadows of observers and a camera crane stretch across the surface near white boundary lines. The perspective shifts slightly, revealing more of the sunlit playing area and the stationary position of the equipment.

A man in a white shirt and blue shorts enters the high-angle shot from the top, walking across the gravel court. Shadows of a camera crane and bystanders stretch across the surface near white boundary lines. Two dark boules remain stationary on the ground as the figure moves through the bright, sunlit scene.

The camera cuts to a low-angle shot showing three men in white shirts walking towards the lens on the sunlit gravel court. One man wears black shorts while another wears blue denim shorts. A large camera crane arm extends overhead against the blue sky and spectator stands. The group walks forward, casting long shadows on the ground.

A man wearing a white cap, white shirt, and blue denim shorts bends forward, holding a green boule in his right hand near his knee. Behind him, another person in a white shirt and black shorts walks across the gravel court. The low-angle shot captures these movements on the sunlit playing surface.

The camera cuts to a wide shot showing a man in a white shirt and black shorts crouching on the left side of the gravel court. Four silver boules and a small jack lie on the ground, separated into two groups. Spectators watch from behind a metal railing in the background under bright sunlight.

The man in the white shirt and black shorts maintains his crouching position on the left side of the court, closely inspecting the layout of the silver boules and jack. The background spectators and metal barriers remain static under the bright sun, with no new objects entering the frame or significant shifts in the scene's composition.

A man in a white shirt and black shorts crouches on the left side of the gravel court. He throws a silver boule which flies through the air and lands near the jack and two other stationary boules on the right side of the frame.

A man in a white shirt and black shorts crouches on the left side of the court after throwing a silver boule. The projectile lands near the jack and two stationary boules on the right side. He remains in a squatting position, watching the metal sphere settle into the cluster on the gravel surface.

The man in the white shirt and black shorts remains crouched on the left side of the court. The silver boule he threw has landed and settled near the jack and other stationary boules on the right. Spectators watch from behind the metal barrier in the background.

A graphic insert displays a close-up of a man with long curly hair and a beard. Text overlays read "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1994" and "LUCIEN JOURNOUD" in bold white letters. Animated yellow, black, and white chevrons slide on the right, with channel logos visible in the top corners.

A graphic overlay continues displaying a close-up of a man with long curly hair and a beard. White text identifies the event as 'MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1994' and the individual as 'LUCIEN JOURNOUD'. Animated yellow, black, and white chevrons decorate the right side, while channel logos remain visible in the top corners.

The graphic overlay vanishes, revealing archival footage of a man with long hair and a beard walking on a gravel court. He wears a dark shirt and light trousers. Several metal boules rest on the ground in the foreground, while spectators sit in tiered stands behind a barrier in the background.

The graphic overlay vanishes, revealing archival footage of a man with long hair and a beard walking on a gravel court. He wears a dark shirt and light trousers. Several metal boules rest on the ground in the foreground, while spectators sit in tiered stands behind a barrier in the background.

The camera cuts to a high-angle close-up of the light gravel surface, revealing three silver metal boules and a small red jack resting on the ground. The man previously walking is no longer visible, leaving the focus entirely on the game pieces scattered on the textured court.

A static high-angle shot focuses on the light gravel surface where three silver metal boules and a small red jack rest motionless. The arrangement of the game pieces remains unchanged, with no players or movement visible in this close-up view of the textured ground.

The high-angle shot remains static on the light gravel surface, showing three silver metal boules and a small red jack resting motionless. The arrangement of the game pieces is unchanged from the previous seconds. The white "ZONE PETANQUE" logo persists in the top left corner against the textured ground.

The static high-angle view continues to display the light gravel court with three silver boules and a red jack lying still. No movement occurs among the game pieces or players. The 'ZONE PETANQUE' logo remains visible in the upper left corner throughout this stationary shot of the playing surface.

The video cuts from the stationary boules to a medium shot of a man with long curly hair and a beard wearing a patterned reddish-brown shirt walking past metal scaffolding. A vertical white banner displaying the text 'Marseillaise' is visible on the right, while another person in a red cap briefly enters the frame.

A man with long dark hair and a beard, dressed in a reddish-brown patterned shirt, walks past metal scaffolding and a vertical white banner reading "Marseillaise". Another man wearing a red cap and a matching patterned shirt enters from the left, walking alongside him near the structures and buildings in the background.

The scene cuts from the men walking near scaffolding to a wide shot of the gravel court. A man with long hair and a beard, wearing a dark shirt and light trousers, walks on the court near scattered metal boules. Spectators sit in tiered stands behind advertising boards in the background.

A man with long hair and a beard, dressed in a dark shirt and light trousers, walks forward on the sunlit gravel court. Several metal boules rest on the ground in the foreground, while spectators watch from tiered stands behind advertising boards in the background.

A man with long hair walks forward on the gravel court. A graphic overlay with gold and black chevrons appears on the left side of the screen. White text displaying "MASSONI" and "95" is visible on the graphic. Several metal boules lie stationary on the ground in the foreground.

A graphic overlay with gold and black chevrons expands on the left, revealing a close-up photo of a man's face and white text reading "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1995" and "ANDRE MASSONI". Behind the graphic, the long-haired man continues walking on the gravel court near stationary metal boules.

A graphic overlay with gold, black, and white chevrons displays a close-up of a man's face and text reading "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1995" and "ANDRE MASSONI". Behind this graphic, a long-haired man in a dark shirt and light trousers walks on the gravel court near stationary metal boules.

The graphic overlay vanishes, cutting to a man in a white shirt and dark trousers leaning forward to throw a metal boule. He extends his right arm downward in a follow-through motion while spectators watch from the dimly lit background stands. The setting changes from a sunny court to a darker environment.

The view switches to a wide shot of a brightly lit gravel court. A man in a white shirt and dark trousers stands on the far right with arms extended outward. Several metal boules lie scattered on the ground in the foreground and middle distance. Spectators are visible behind a low barrier in the background.

The video cuts to a medium close-up of a man with short light hair wearing a white button-down shirt. He stands facing slightly left with a neutral expression against a dark background featuring blurred spectators and lights. The scene transitions from a wide court view to this focused shot.

A medium close-up shows a man with short hair in a white shirt against a dark background. A graphic overlay appears featuring gold, black, and white chevrons on the right side. White text reads "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2019" above the name "THIERRY PASTORE" in bold letters at the bottom.

A graphic overlay persists with gold, black, and white chevrons on the right. White text reads "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2019" above "THIERRY PASTORE" in bold letters. On the left, a close-up shows a man with short hair and a serious expression looking forward, while the "ZONE PETANQUE" logo remains in the top left corner.

The video cuts to a man in a beige polo shirt with a number 3 walking forward on a gravel court. Spectators sit behind a silver metal barrier and a blue banner reading 'france'. A scoreboard in the top left displays scores 7 and 2, while a channel logo appears in the top right corner.

A man in a beige polo shirt with the number 3 walks forward on a gravel court. Spectators sit behind metal barriers and a blue banner. He bends forward at the waist, swinging his right arm back as he prepares to throw, looking down intently at the ground.

The view switches to a wide shot of the gravel court showing a silver boule and red jack in the foreground. The player in the beige shirt stands further back near the center, while a cameraman kneels on the left side near blue advertising boards and spectators watch from the tiered stands in the background.

The scene cuts to a close-up of the player in the beige shirt, leaning forward with arms raised and mouth open. Spectators behind him watch intently, with one person clapping. The number 3 is visible on his chest. The background shows tiered seating filled with people.

The man in the beige polo shirt with the number 3 lowers his raised arms and turns his body slightly to the right. A second person wearing a matching beige shirt enters the frame on the right edge. Spectators remain seated behind the silver metal barrier in the background.

The man in the beige polo shirt with the number 3 walks forward in the foreground. Another person in a matching beige shirt is visible on the right edge. Spectators sit behind a metal barrier in the background. A large green blur obscures the right side of the frame.

The scene cuts to a wide shot showing three men in beige shirts walking down the gravel court towards the foreground. Two wear white caps. Spectators sit in bleachers behind blue advertising boards and a white tent. In the foreground, a silver boule and red jack rest on the ground near the white boundary line.

Three men in beige shirts walk toward the camera on the gravel court; the leftmost wears black shorts with vertical white text "MONACO". A person in a bright yellow shirt walks away on the far left. The central player holds a metal boule as spectators watch from bleachers behind blue advertising boards.

The scene cuts to a graphic overlay featuring a close-up of a man with a mustache wearing a patterned shirt. White text displays "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1994" above the name "ATTILIO CATOIO" in bold letters. Gold, black, and white chevron shapes decorate the right side of the screen.

The graphic overlay transitions to live footage showing a man with a mustache in a reddish-brown patterned shirt and light trousers with a belt. He stands facing forward on the court, holding a metal boule with both hands near his waist, while blurred spectators sit in the background.

A man with a mustache wearing a reddish-brown patterned shirt and light trousers stands on the court holding a metal boule with both hands near his waist. He faces forward while blurred spectators sit in the tiered stands behind him.

A man with a mustache wearing a reddish-brown patterned shirt stands facing forward on the court. He holds a metal boule with both hands near his waist while blurred spectators sit in the tiered stands behind him. His posture remains steady as he looks ahead.

A man with a mustache wearing a reddish-brown patterned shirt stands facing forward on the court, holding a metal boule with both hands near his waist. Blurred spectators sit in the tiered stands behind him as he maintains a steady posture, looking ahead while preparing for his turn.

The man in the reddish-brown patterned shirt remains standing on the court, gripping the metal boule with both hands near his waist. He maintains a focused expression looking forward, while the blurred spectators in the background stay seated in the tiered stands behind him. The lighting highlights the pattern on his shirt.

The view switches to a wide shot of the gravel court where three metal boules and a red jack lie scattered. A person wearing light trousers stands on the far right edge. Background advertising boards display logos like "OBUT" and "ORANGINA" before the seated spectators.

The scene remains a wide shot of the gravel court with three metal boules. Two boules are clustered on the left and one is on the right. The red jack is no longer visible in the central area. A person in light trousers stands on the far right. Background advertising boards with 'OBUT' and 'ORANGINA' are visible behind the spectators.

A person wearing dark pants moves into the immediate foreground from the right side, significantly obstructing the camera view. Their dark silhouette fills most of the frame. Behind them, three metal boules remain stationary on the light-colored gravel court near the distant advertising boards.

A figure in dark trousers steps directly into the foreground, blocking almost the entire camera view of the gravel court and boules. A scoreboard graphic overlays the bottom of the screen, listing ZERDOUMI with 2 points and TUPPO with 8 points in white text against a dark background.

The scoreboard graphic listing player scores vanishes as the scene dissolves into a blurry, abstract transition. A dark, vertical shape moves centrally against a light, indistinct background, obscuring the previous court view. This visual shifts rapidly into a textured silhouette graphic overlay featuring faint, translucent sphere shapes against a gray gravel texture.

A dark, grainy silhouette of a figure in a throwing stance dominates the frame, superimposed over a light gray gravel background. Faint, translucent images of metal boules are visible within the dark figure's form and to the left. The graphic overlay remains largely static throughout the duration, maintaining the textured appearance.

The dark, grainy silhouette of a figure remains superimposed over the light gray gravel background. Faint, translucent images of metal boules are visible within the dark form and to the left side. The graphic overlay stays static with no significant movement or changes in the textured appearance during this interval.

The visual remains a static graphic overlay showing a dark, grainy silhouette of a figure against a light gray gravel texture. Faint, translucent images of metal boules appear within the dark form and to the left, with no significant movement or changes occurring.

The visual remains a static graphic overlay showing a dark, grainy silhouette of a figure against a light gray gravel texture. Faint, translucent images of metal boules appear within the dark form and to the left, with no significant movement or changes occurring.

The scene continues to display a static graphic overlay with a dark, grainy silhouette of a figure superimposed on a light gray gravel background. Translucent images of metal boules are visible within the silhouette and to the left, remaining unchanged with no new movement or visual elements introduced.

The visual remains a static graphic overlay featuring a dark, grainy silhouette of a figure centered against a light gray gravel background. Faint, translucent images of metal boules persist within the silhouette's form and to the left, with no movement or new visual elements introduced during this interval.

Faint, translucent images of metal boules persist within the silhouette's form and to the left, with no movement or new visual elements introduced during this interval.

summary.text: [0-2] Several small translucent spheres rest on gray gravel before a large dark jagged mass impacts the foreground, superimposed with a yellow graphical overlay. [2-6] White text "La chaine YouTube" displays above a yellow line while "Zone Pétanque présente" appears below; a large dark sphere rolls in from the right. [6-8] The text and line fade as a dark silhouette of a person solidifies in the center, facing away, with a yellow vertical bar on the right edge. [8-10] The bar disappears, the silhouette stands with hands on hips, and large white text "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" overlays the scene. [10-12] The main text undergoes a glitch effect, smaller text "très précis" fades in centrally, and the silhouette leans forward against the gravel background.

[12-14] A dark silhouette of a figure in a cap is captured mid-throw against gray gravel. Glitched white text reading "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" and "très précis" is superimposed.
[14-16] The text changes to bold white "TIRS DE RAFI" centered over the figure's upper body silhouette, with a yellow vertical bar on the left.
[16-20] A close-up of a man's face appears beside yellow, black, and white chevrons. A banner displays "NORDINE ZOUAK" while faint text reads "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2004" and a "ZONE PETANQUE" logo is top left.
[20-22] The view shifts to a high-angle shot of a gravel court showing three silver boules and a small green jack near a white line.
[22-24] Finally, a medium shot shows a man in a light tan shirt and gold necklace standing before blurred spectators, with the logo remaining visible.

[24-26] A medium shot features a man in a light tan button-down shirt and gold necklace facing forward against blurred spectators, with the "ZONE PETANQUE" logo top left. [26-30] The scene cuts to a wide angle of the pétanque court, revealing scattered metal boules on the sand near a white line and a blue "Marionnaud" banner behind spectators lining a metal barrier. [30-32] The view settles on a white boundary corner where two silver boules lie near two standing observers, one in dark shorts and sandals, the other in brown shorts. [32-36] Finally, a man in a green shirt and dark shorts walks away across the gravel court past a dense crowd and a banner partially reading "La Marseill," while the channel logo remains visible.

[38-40] A close-up shows a man in a light tan shirt leaning forward with a fixed gaze, preparing to throw against a background of blurred spectators, with the white "ZONE PETANQUE" logo in the upper left. [40-42] He extends his arm to finish the motion, cutting to a high-angle gravel court view where three silver boules and a small green jack lie near a white line. [42-44] A high-angle view shows a new silver boule rolling into the frame from the top right area, moving toward the center cluster. [44-46] The silver boule rolls rapidly across the upper right section, and the upper area becomes empty as the top boule is knocked away, leaving the green jack and two stationary boules near the bottom line. [46-48] A static high-angle shot displays the gravel court with the green jack near the line and two silver boules at the bottom edge, the upper section clear, and the logo visible.

[36-38] A man in a green shirt walks right across the gravel court, while a man in a white t-shirt and dark shorts walks left near the barrier before a cross-fade superimposes a face. [48-52] A close-up shows a man in dark sunglasses as yellow, black, and white chevron graphics slide in. Text reads "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2020" and "PATRICE GREGORI" with the "ZONE PETANQUE" logo visible. [52-54] The scene cuts to a wide shot of a sunlit court with spectators in bleachers. Three players in white shirts and shorts stand on the gravel—one left, two right—near metal boules. A scoreboard is top left and a channel logo is top right.

[58-62] A close-up captures a man with a grey beard and dark sunglasses looking downward, dressed in a white collared shirt. The "ZONE PETANQUE" logo appears in the top left corner, and a channel logo with the number "3" is visible in the top right against a blurred background. [62-66] The view switches to a wide shot of a sunlit gravel court where a man in a white shirt and dark shorts crouches on the left side. Two men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right, while three metal boules lie on the ground in the foreground. Spectators occupy tiered seating in the background under a bright sky.

[66-70] A high-angle view shows a gray gravel court with white boundary lines and two dark metal boules resting on the ground. Shadows of observers and a camera crane stretch across the sunlit surface, with "ZONE PETANQUE" and "3" logos visible. [70-72] A man in a white shirt and blue shorts walks across the gravel court near the stationary boules, passing under the shadow of the camera equipment. [72-74] The scene cuts to a low-angle shot of men in white shirts walking toward the camera; one wears black shorts and another wears blue denim shorts. A camera crane arm is visible overhead against a blue sky and spectator stands as the group walks forward, casting long shadows.

[76-80] Under bright sunlight, a man in a white shirt and black shorts crouches on the left side of a gravel court, inspecting silver boules and a small jack separated into groups. Spectators watch from behind a metal railing in the background, with "ZONE PETANQUE" and channel "3" logos visible. [80-84] The man throws a silver boule that flies through the air, landing near the jack and two stationary boules on the right side. He maintains his squatting position to watch the sphere settle into the cluster. Afterwards, he stands up and walks forward as the partial text "OUD" appears on screen.

[84-86] A man in a white shirt and black shorts remains crouched on the left side of the court. The silver boule he threw has landed and settled near the jack and other stationary boules on the right. Spectators watch from behind the metal barrier in the background. [86-90] A graphic insert displays a close-up of a man with long curly hair and a beard. Text overlays read "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1994" and "LUCIEN JOURNOUD" in bold white letters. Animated yellow, black, and white chevrons slide on the right, with "ZONE PETANQUE" logo visible in the top left corner. [90-92] The graphic overlay vanishes, revealing archival footage of a man with long hair and a beard walking on a gravel court. He wears a dark shirt and light trousers. Several metal boules rest on the ground in the foreground, while spectators sit in tiered stands behind a barrier and advertising boards in the background.

[92-94] A man with long hair and a beard, wearing a dark shirt and light trousers, walks on a gravel court. Metal boules rest on the ground in the foreground, while spectators sit in tiered stands behind a barrier. Banners reading "OBUT" and "MGM" are visible in the background, along with the "ZONE PETANQUE" logo.
[94-100] The camera cuts to a static high-angle close-up of the light gravel surface. Three silver metal boules and a small red jack rest motionless. The arrangement remains unchanged, with no players visible. The "ZONE PETANQUE" logo persists in the top left corner against the textured ground.

[100-102] A static high-angle view displays a light gravel court with three silver boules and a red jack lying still, with the 'ZONE PETANQUE' logo visible in the upper left corner. [102-106] The video cuts to a medium shot of a man with long curly hair and a beard wearing a patterned reddish-brown shirt walking past metal scaffolding and a vertical white banner reading "Marseillaise". Another man in a red cap and matching patterned shirt enters from the left near structures and buildings. [106-108] The scene cuts to a wide shot of the gravel court where a man with long hair and a beard, wearing a dark shirt and light trousers, walks near scattered metal boules while spectators sit in tiered stands behind advertising boards.

[108-110] A bearded man with long hair, wearing a dark shirt and light trousers, walks forward on a sunlit gravel court where metal boules rest in the foreground, while spectators watch from tiered stands behind advertising boards. [110-112] As the man walks, a graphic overlay with gold and black chevrons appears on the left, showing text "MASSONI" and "95" near the stationary boules. [112-116] The graphic expands to display a close-up face and text "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1995" and "ANDRE MASSONI" in white, overlaying the scene where the long-haired player continues walking on the court near the metal balls.

[116-118] A man in a white shirt and dark trousers leans forward to throw a metal boule, extending his right arm downward in a follow-through motion while spectators watch from the dimly lit background stands. [120-122] The video cuts to a medium close-up of a man with short light hair wearing a white button-down shirt facing slightly left with a neutral expression against a dark background featuring blurred spectators and lights. [124-126] A graphic overlay with gold, black, and white chevrons displays "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2019" and "THIERRY PASTORE" next to a close-up of the player, with the "ZONE PETANQUE" logo in the corner. [126-130] The view shifts to Thierry walking on a gravel court wearing a beige polo shirt with the number 3. Spectators sit behind silver metal barriers and a blue banner reading 'france', while a scoreboard shows 7-2 and the "la chaine L'EQUIPE" logo is visible. He bends forward at the waist, swinging his right arm back to prepare a throw. [130-132] A wide shot shows the court with a silver boule and red jack in the foreground. Thierry stands further back, a cameraman kneels on the left near blue advertising boards, and spectators watch from tiered stands.

[56-58] On a sunlit gravel court, a man in a white shirt and dark shorts remains crouched near the left white boundary line while two other men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right. [74-76] A man wearing a white cap, white shirt, and blue denim shorts bends forward, holding a green boule in his right hand near his knee. Behind him, another person in a white shirt and black shorts walks across the court. [132-136] The scene cuts to a close-up of a player in a beige shirt with the number 3 on his chest. He leans forward with arms raised and mouth open, shouting, as spectators watch and clap behind a silver barrier. He then lowers his arms and turns slightly right as a second person in a matching beige shirt enters the frame. Overlay text reads "ZONE PETANQUE", scores "7 2", and "la chaine L'EQUIPE Mondial La Marseillaise à Pétanque 1/4 de finale".

[140-142] Three men in beige shirts walk toward the camera on the gravel court; the leftmost wears black shorts with vertical white text "MONACO". [148-154] A man with a mustache wearing a reddish-brown patterned shirt stands on the court holding a metal boule with both hands near his waist. The man in the beige polo shirt with the number 3 walks forward in the foreground. Another person in a matching beige shirt is visible on the right edge. Spectators sit behind a metal barrier in the background. A large green blur obscures the right side of the frame.

The scene cuts to a wide shot showing three men in beige shirts walking down the gravel court towards the foreground. Two wear white caps. Spectators sit in bleachers behind blue advertising boards and a white tent. In the foreground, a silver boule and red jack rest on the ground near the white boundary line.

A man with a mustache wearing a reddish-brown patterned shirt stands facing forward on the court. He holds a metal boule with both hands near his waist while blurred spectators sit in the tiered stands behind him. His posture remains steady as he looks ahead.

A man with a mustache wearing a reddish-brown patterned shirt stands facing forward on the court, holding a metal boule with both hands near his waist. Blurred spectators sit in the tiered stands behind him as he maintains a steady posture, looking ahead while preparing for his turn.

The man in the reddish-brown patterned shirt remains standing on the court, gripping the metal boule with both hands near his waist. He maintains a focused expression looking forward, while the blurred spectators in the background stay seated in the tiered stands behind him. The lighting highlights the pattern on his shirt.

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] Several small translucent spheres rest on gray gravel before a large dark jagged mass impacts the foreground, superimposed with a yellow graphical overlay. [2-6] White text "La chaine YouTube" displays above a yellow line while "Zone Pétanque présente" appears below; a large dark sphere rolls in from the right. [6-8] The text and line fade as a dark silhouette of a person solidifies in the center, facing away, with a yellow vertical bar on the right edge. [8-10] The bar disappears, the silhouette stands with hands on hips, and large white text "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" overlays the scene. [10-12] The main text undergoes a glitch effect, smaller text "très précis" fades in centrally, and the silhouette leans forward against the gravel background. [12-14] A dark silhouette of a figure in a cap is captured mid-throw against gray gravel. Glitched white text reading "6 TIRS DE RAFLE" and "très précis" is superimposed. [14-16] The text changes to bold white "TIRS DE RAFI" centered over the figure's upper body silhouette, with a yellow vertical bar on the left. [16-20] A close-up of a man's face appears beside yellow, black, and white chevrons. A banner displays "NORDINE ZOUAK" while faint text

[116-118] A man in a white shirt and dark trousers leans forward to throw a metal boule, extending his right arm downward in a follow-through motion while spectators watch from the dimly lit background stands. [118-120] The view switches to a wide shot of a brightly lit gravel court where a man in a white shirt and dark trousers stands on the far right with arms extended outward, while several metal boules lie scattered on the ground and spectators are visible behind a low barrier. [120-122] The video cuts to a medium close-up of a man with short light hair wearing a white button-down shirt facing slightly left with a neutral expression against a dark background featuring blurred spectators and lights. [122-124] A medium close-up shows a man with short hair in a white shirt against a dark background as a graphic overlay appears featuring gold, black, and white chevrons on the right side with white text reading "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2019" above the name "THIERRY PASTORE" in bold letters at the bottom.

[124-126] A graphic overlay with gold, black, and white chevrons displays "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 2019" and "THIERRY PASTORE" next to a close-up of the player, with the "ZONE PETANQUE" logo in the corner. [126-130] The view shifts to Thierry walking on a gravel court wearing a beige polo shirt with the number 3. Spectators sit behind silver metal barriers and a blue banner reading 'france', while a scoreboard shows 7-2 and the "la chaine L'EQUIPE" logo is visible. He bends forward at the waist, swinging his right arm back to prepare a throw. [130-132] A wide shot shows the court with a silver boule and red jack in the foreground. Thierry stands further back, a cameraman kneels on the left near blue advertising boards, and spectators watch from tiered stands.

[56-58] On a sunlit gravel court, a man in a white shirt and dark shorts remains crouched near the left white boundary line while two other men in white shirts and shorts stand observing on the right. Three metal boules rest on the ground as spectators fill the tiered seating in the background. [74-76] A man wearing a white cap, white shirt, and blue denim shorts bends forward, holding a green boule in his right hand near his knee. Behind him, another person in a white shirt and black shorts walks across the court. [132-136] The scene cuts to a close-up of a player in a beige shirt with the number 3 on his chest. He leans forward with arms raised and mouth open, shouting, as spectators watch and clap behind a silver barrier. He then lowers his arms and turns slightly right as a second person in a matching beige shirt enters the frame. Overlay text reads "ZONE PETANQUE", scores "7 2", and "la chaine L'EQUIPE Mondial La Marseillaise à Pétanque 1/4 de finale".

[140-142] Three men in beige shirts walk toward the camera on the gravel court; the leftmost wears black shorts with vertical white text "MONACO". A person in a bright yellow shirt walks away on the far left. The central player holds a metal boule as spectators watch from bleachers behind blue advertising boards. [142-144] The scene cuts to a graphic overlay featuring a close-up of a man with a mustache wearing a patterned shirt. White text displays "MONDIAL LA MARSEILLAISE 1994" above the name "ATTILIO CATOIO" in bold letters. Gold, black, and white chevron shapes decorate the right side of the screen. [144-148] The graphic transitions to live footage showing the man with a mustache in a reddish-brown patterned shirt and light trousers with a belt standing facing forward on the court, holding a metal boule with both hands near his waist, while blurred spectators sit in the background tiered stands.
